construct.label: Epistemic trust anxiety
construct.intended_use: Development-only Wave 6 calibration record for the four-item epistemic trust anxiety construct candidate, covering anxiety about AI-mediated evidence authenticity, news provenance, expert-claim conflict, and personalized misinformation.

development_pilot.sampling_frame: United States adults aged 18 or older eligible for the Wave 6 epistemic trust calibration survey.
development_pilot.recruitment_source: Professional online panel or probability-based panel with documented quota, weighting, duplicate-detection, AI-exposure, media-exposure, and information-environment role variables.
development_pilot.exclusion_summary: No responses have been collected. Exclusions will follow the Wave 6 preregistered consent, eligibility, duplicate, attention-check, scenario-comprehension, epistemic-attribution, speeding, missingness, straightlining, and self-reported-understanding rules.
development_pilot.provenance_notes: The development pilot is reserved for item distribution review, ordinal EFA, preliminary omega, early IRT diagnostics, implementation checks, and retention recommendations specified in analysis/v0.6/epistemic_trust/wave6_analysis_plan.json.

confirmation_sample.sampling_frame: Independent United States adult respondent pool with no respondent overlap with the development pilot.
confirmation_sample.recruitment_source: Separate panel batch or probability-based sample source documented before confirmation analysis, with the same quota, weighting, subgroup, and restricted operations fields defined in the Wave 6 codebook.
confirmation_sample.fielding_dates: Planned after development-pilot implementation checks and before confirmation outcome inspection
confirmation_sample.exclusion_summary: No confirmation responses have been collected. All preregistered quality-control and eligibility exclusions will be applied before CFA, reliability, IRT, DIF, invariance, criterion-validity, behavioral-validity, and incremental-validity analyses.
confirmation_sample.provenance_notes: The confirmation sample is required before any item-level or construct-level epistemic trust anxiety scoring can be considered in a later citable release.

refresh_or_bridge_sample.sampling_frame: No scored epistemic item version exists yet, so no refresh or bridge sample is required for v0.6.0.
refresh_or_bridge_sample.recruitment_source: Not applicable until item wording, response anchors, language, administration mode, platform labeling, or scoring rules are revised after observed validation.
refresh_or_bridge_sample.fielding_dates: Not applicable for the v0.6.0 development-only freeze
refresh_or_bridge_sample.exclusion_summary: No bridge exclusions are applicable because no scored baseline epistemic item set exists.
refresh_or_bridge_sample.provenance_notes: Any future claim of comparability across revised epistemic item wording, languages, administration modes, or platform provenance labels will require a separate preregistered bridge dossier.

efa.method: Ordinal exploratory factor analysis using polychoric correlations, oblique rotation, and parallel analysis as specified in analysis/v0.6/epistemic_trust/wave6_analysis_plan.json.
efa.decision_rule: Retain items only when the preregistered factor structure is interpretable, primary loadings are at least 0.50, cross-loading behavior is acceptable, response spread is usable, and retained content spans distinct epistemic trust mechanisms.
efa.result_summary: No Wave 6 development-pilot responses have been collected; EFA evidence is pending and cannot support scoring.
efa.release_consequence: All four epistemic items remain development_only and excluded from official ANX-Bench scoring in v0.6.0.

cfa.method: Ordered-categorical confirmatory factor analysis using WLSMV for the preregistered one-factor epistemic_trust_anxiety model after development-pilot retention decisions.
cfa.decision_rule: Strong support requires CFI and TLI at least 0.95, RMSEA at most 0.06, SRMR at most 0.08, and standardized loadings at least 0.50; caution ranges and fail rules follow the Wave 6 preregistration.
cfa.result_summary: No independent Wave 6 confirmation sample has been fielded; CFA evidence is unavailable.
cfa.release_consequence: Construct, domain, overall, longitudinal, event-study, cross-domain, individual-level, and clinical scoring claims remain prohibited.

reliability.method: Ordinal omega as the primary reliability statistic, with coefficient alpha, corrected item-total correlations, missingness diagnostics, and sensitivity to removing each retained item.
reliability.decision_rule: Early scored-construct review requires omega at least 0.70, corrected item-total correlations at least 0.30, no unacceptable missingness, and no item whose removal materially improves the retained scale.
reliability.result_summary: No Wave 6 reliability estimates exist because calibration responses have not been collected.
reliability.release_consequence: The dossier cannot support an internally reliable epistemic trust anxiety score in v0.6.0.

irt.method: Graded-response IRT calibration estimating discrimination, ordered category thresholds, item information, response-pattern fit, and residual local dependence.
irt.decision_rule: Retain items only if discrimination is at least 0.65, thresholds are ordered or substantively justified, item information is useful in the observed trait range, and local dependence does not collapse distinct epistemic mechanisms.
irt.result_summary: No IRT model has been estimated for Wave 6.
irt.release_consequence: No IRT theta scoring, linking, item-information claim, or adaptive-use claim is permitted for the v0.6.0 packet.

dif.method: Ordinal logistic DIF screening with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control plus IRT DIF sensitivity where subgroup sizes permit, covering demographic, AI-exposure, news-exposure, media-literacy, institutional-trust, information-role, baseline-anxiety, and device groups.
dif.decision_rule: Any statistically supported and practically meaningful unresolved DIF blocks scored use for affected items or subgroup comparison claims.
dif.result_summary: No Wave 6 DIF results exist because the confirmation sample has not been analyzed.
dif.release_consequence: The item pool cannot support subgroup comparisons, weighted comparison claims, or event-study heterogeneity claims.

invariance.method: Multi-group ordered-categorical CFA with configural, metric, and scalar or threshold tests, with preregistered IRT linking fallback if CFA is unstable.
invariance.decision_rule: Latent mean, longitudinal, cross-group, or event-window comparisons require scalar or threshold invariance, or a preregistered validated linking alternative, for the relevant groups.
invariance.result_summary: No Wave 6 invariance evidence has been produced.
invariance.release_consequence: The dossier permits development administration only and prohibits benchmark comparison claims.

convergent.method: Spearman correlations and ordinal models relating retained epistemic scores to perceived AI information exposure and AI information uncertainty worry after dimensionality and reliability gates pass.
convergent.decision_rule: Convergent support requires positive associations in preregistered practical ranges without substituting criterion evidence for psychometric approval.
convergent.result_summary: No convergent-validity analysis has been run.
convergent.release_consequence: No external-validity claim is permitted in v0.6.0.

discriminant.method: Association checks against the baseline two-item general anxiety mean to verify AI-specific epistemic anxiety is not redundant with broad anxiety.
discriminant.decision_rule: A correlation above 0.70 blocks scored approval unless the dossier demonstrates preregistered incremental AI-specific variance.
discriminant.result_summary: No discriminant-validity analysis has been run.
discriminant.release_consequence: No discriminant-validity claim is permitted in v0.6.0.

criterion.method: Ordinal regression and behavioral criterion models for AI information uncertainty worry, authenticity verification burden, news verification avoidance, and the revealed human verification allocation task.
criterion.decision_rule: Criterion support requires preregistered positive associations of practical size after psychometric retention and cannot by itself authorize scoring.
criterion.result_summary: No criterion-validity or behavioral-validity analysis has been run.
criterion.release_consequence: No criterion-validity, behavioral-validity, or predictive claim is permitted in v0.6.0.

incremental_validity.method: Adjusted models testing whether retained epistemic scores add explanatory value beyond demographics, AI exposure, AI-news exposure, media literacy, institutional trust, political information exposure, baseline general anxiety, and information-environment role.
incremental_validity.decision_rule: Incremental validity requires at least one preregistered criterion to show stable incremental signal after covariate adjustment.
incremental_validity.result_summary: No incremental-validity analysis has been run.
incremental_validity.release_consequence: No claim that epistemic_trust_anxiety adds unique explanatory value is permitted in v0.6.0.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers anxiety about contested digital evidence authenticity when AI detection systems and expert analyses disagree.
retention_table.threshold: The candidate pool must include evidence-authenticity anxiety without reducing the construct to general media skepticism.
retention_table.rationale: The item is substantively within scope, but no Wave 6 EFA, CFA, reliability, IRT, DIF, invariance, or validity evidence has been collected.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers anxiety about provenance uncertainty when synthetic and authentic news artifacts are mixed in a fragmented public information stream.
retention_table.threshold: The candidate pool must represent source-provenance uncertainty as distinct from deepfake evidence disputes.
retention_table.rationale: The item has relevant construct coverage, but retention for scoring awaits preregistered Wave 6 psychometric and validity evidence.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers anxiety about conflicting AI-supported expert conclusions that ordinary residents cannot adjudicate before acting on public guidance.
retention_table.threshold: The candidate pool must include expert-authority conflict as a distinct epistemic trust mechanism.
retention_table.rationale: The item remains development-only until the Wave 6 pilot and confirmation samples show acceptable loading, reliability, subgroup behavior, and criterion pattern.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers anxiety about AI-personalized misinformation that becomes difficult to recognize because each recipient receives a tailored variant.
retention_table.threshold: The candidate pool must include individualized information manipulation as a distinct mechanism affecting trust in shared information environments.
retention_table.rationale: The item is substantively necessary but lacks observed Wave 6 evidence needed for item retention, subgroup comparability, or scored release.

decision.approval_scope: Development administration only for the Wave 6 epistemic calibration packet; no official item-level, construct, domain, overall, longitudinal, event-study, cross-domain, clinical, diagnostic, individual-level, or policy-decision scoring.
decision.rationale: This dossier reserves the Wave 6 validation record and points to the frozen Wave 6 preregistration and analysis plan, but no observed calibration sample has been fielded, no psychometric evidence has been reviewed, and no reviewer has approved scoring.

reviewer_signoff.notes: Development-only planning dossier for docs/preregistrations/anx_us_2026w06_epistemic_calibration.md and analysis/v0.6/epistemic_trust/wave6_analysis_plan.json; it does not approve scored benchmark use.
